sutta: and 11 now casey the horse trainer came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him he saluted the exalted 1 and sat down at 1 side as he said thus the exalted 1 said this to casey the horse trainer you yourself casey are a trained man a trainer of horses for driving now casey pray how do you train a man attainable horse and he said as for me your honor i train attainable horse by mildness also by harshness also by both mildness and harshness but suppose casey the buddha said a tameable horse and does not submit to your training by mildness nor to the training by harshness nor yet to the training by both methods what do you do in such a case your honor i destroy him why so with the idea let him not be a discredit to my teacher's clan however your honor the exalted 1 is unsurpassed as a trainer of men for driving pray your honor how does the exalted 1 train a tameable man
commentary:
i'll just stop here for a while to make a comment here casey he says he's trained his horses by mildness that means a soft approach and then by harshness means a hard approach like maybe the mild approach is maybe he gives sweets or something that the horse likes to eat so that the horse will listen to him and the harshness uh maybe if the horse doesn't listen maybe he'll grip the horse and if using both these methods and the horse still cannot be trained then he says he destroys the horse he kills the horse because he doesn't want his reputation to be spoiled or his teacher's reputation to be spotless so now he asked the buddha how does the buddha train a tameable man then the buddha answered for my part kc i too trained a tameable man by mildness also by harshness also by both together this casey is the way of mildness thus is good conduct in body thus is the result of good conduct in body thus is good conduct in speech thus is the good result of good conduct in speech thus is good conduct in mind thus is the result of good conduct in mind thus our devas and thus are men and this kc is the way of harshness thus is bad conduct in body thus is the result of bad conduct in body thus is bad conduct in speech thus is the result of bad conduct in speech thus is bad conduct in mind thus is the result of bad conduct in mind thus thus is the health realm thus is the animal realm thus is the realm of goals and this casey is the way of both mildness and harshness thus is good conduct in body speech and mind thus is the result thereof thus is bad conduct in body speech and mind thus is the result thereof thus our devas and men thus is the hell brahm thus is the animal realm thus is the realm of course i'll stop for a while here to explain a bit so here the buddha is saying that using the soft approach the mild approach he he teaches that good conduct of body speech and mind will result in being born as a deva or being born as a as men in the human realm and then the way of harshness the heart approach is to teach that by um by bad conduct in body speech and mind the 3 karmas we can be reborn in the hell realm the animal realm or the realm of ghouls 1 thing you notice in the sutas in our our original phone nikayasa that when the buddha talks about the woeful planes is always tree you know the hell realm the animal realm and the ghost realm the in the sutas and the original sutas the asura plains are not considered a woeful plane the asuras are divas heavenly beings it's only the later books that put the asurasa into the woeful plains then casey said but your honor if the man for training won't submit to the training by mildness by harshness nor to the 2 together pray what does the exalted 1 do in such a case casey i destroy him then casey said but surely the exalted 1 does not take life and yet the exalted 1 spoke thus i destroy him casey true it is casey that taking life does not become of a tatar gather yet if the man to be trained submits not to the training by mildness by harshness or both together then the tattagata thinks it not worthwhile to admonish that man nor do his wise fellows in the holy life think it worthwhile to admonish that man this casey is destruction for a man in the discipline of the aryan when both the tatar gather and his fellows in the holy life think it not worthwhile to admonish him and casey said destroyed indeed your honor is a man by the welfare when both the tata gata and his fellows in the holy life think it not worthwhile to admonish him it is wonderful your honor it is marvelous may the exalted 1 accept me as a follower from this day forth as long as life lasts as 1 who has taken refuge with him so in this the last partner the buddha says that the person that a man who does not submit to the training he does not listen to advice then the buddha will not give him the advice will not give give him the teaching and this is considered death in the aryan discipline why death because he cannot progress if he is not given advice he is not given teaching by the buddha or by his teacher that means he cannot progress spiritually and that is death for him in the aryan discipline it is also considered a death in the aryan discipline when among these ropes goes back to the low life that is also a death in the aryan discipline also when he breaks 1 of the 1 of the major precepts when he breaks 1 of the major precepts that means the paradigm the very heavy precepts if he breaks it now then he's considered normal among even though he wears the rope i think that's um that is there's more correct rather than just to say that he disrobed that he's actually fallen into the graves offense for among and then this room so in the in the spiritual life it is a teacher is is considered very important if we don't have a teacher to learn under and then we cannot progress and the buddha said that if your teacher guides you so that your sila samadhi and panya increase then you should follow him for the rest of your life and buddha sit somewhere in 1 of the sutasla but if he seela samadhi and panya is worse than yours lower than yours then you should run away from him and this stated in 1 of another suta so you see this another 1 of those where the buddha used a similar in a kind of humorous way to to teach the dhamma

come to the next suta 4.12.100 and 14 monks possessed of 4 qualities a rajas elephant is worthy of the rajya a possession of the raja is reckoned an attribute of a raja what are the 4 qualities here in monks raja's elephant is a listener a destroyer a bearer a goer and how monks is a rajas elephant a listener in this case monks whatever tasks the trainer of elephants imposes on him whether he has performed it before or not the rajas elephant makes that his object gives attention to it considers it with all his mind with ready ear listens there too thus monks is a raja's elephant a listener and how monks is a raja's elephant a destroyer in this case monks raja's elephant entering battle destroys elephant and mahud horse and rider chariot and driver and foot man thus is he a destroyer and how monks is a raja's elephant a bearer in this case monks a raja's elephant entering battle bears the blows of spear sword arrow and axe and also the ding of drum and casual drum of conch temtem and other noise thus he is a bearer and how monks is a raja's elephant a goer in this case amongst a raja's elephant in whatever direction the trainer of elephants turns him whether he has gone there before or not there he quickly goes thus he is a goer so possessed of these 4 qualities monks raja's elephant is worthy of the rajya a possession of the rajya is reckoned an attribute of araja in like men and monks possessed of 4 qualities among is worshipful worthy of gifts and offerings of salutations with claps hands a field of merit unsurpassed for the world what for here in monks a mom is a listener a destroyer a bearer and a grower and how is among a listener in this case when dhamma vinaya is set forth by a tata gata a monk makes that his object gives attention to it considers it with all his mind with ready ear listens to dharma thus is among a listener and how is among a destroyer in this case a monk does not admit sensual thinking that has arisen he abandons restraints makes an end of it forces it not to recur so also with regard to malicious thinking cruel or harmful thinking he does not admit evil and profitable states that occur from time to time he abandons them restrains makes an end of them forces them not to recur thus he is a destroyer and how is among a bearer in this case a man among bears heat cold hunger thirst contact of flies mosquitoes wind and sun and creeping things he bears abusive pain crossing ways of speech he submits to painful bodily feelings grievous sharp wrecking distracting and discovering discomforting that drain the life away thus is among a bearer and how is among a goer in this case among quickly goes where in this long journey he has never gone before namely to the tranquilization of the conditioner sankara to the forsaking of every basis of rebirth to the destruction of craving to passionate passionlessness to ending tunibana thus is among agor possessed of these 4 qualities among is worshipful worthy of gifts and offerings of salutations with claps hands a field of marriage unsurpassed for the world in this suit number 114 the buddha compared rajya's elephant to a monk this is 1 of the sutas where the buddha uses this comparison of the naga an elephant with a monk this word naga denotes an elephant it also means a snake spirit a snake deva a deva with a snake body it also mean is used for a great being a noble person in this suta the buddha said that raja an elephant is worthy of raja if it possesses 4 qualities namely that it is a listener a destroyer a bearer and a goer and similarly if a monk possessed 4 qualities then he is worshipful worthy of gifts and offerings worthy of salutations with clasp hands a field of merit unsurpassed for the world and these 4 qualities are that the monk is a listener a destroyer a bearer and a goal and the buddha said in this case being a listener means that the monk listens to dhamma vinaya the teachings of the buddha the discourses and the disciplinary conduct the disciplinary code set forth by the buddha and buddha's disciple is known as a sabaka the buddha called his disciples savakas savaka means literally means a listener a hearer 1 who listens to the buddha's teachings and so you can see that listening learning the buddha's teachings is very important another word that is mentioned quite often in the discourses is bahu sacha which means much learning uh much learning mean much learning of the dhamma with naya and um which means much knowledge of the teachings of the buddha so this is 1 of the much learning much knowledge of the dhamma is 1 of the key stones foundation of the spiritual path and the second quality is that the monk is a destroyer which means that he destroys sensual thinking malicious thinking and cruel or harmful thinking these are the wrong thoughts that should be destroyed by a monk and the third is that the monk is a bearer he bears heat and coal hungers contact with flies mosquitoes wind and sun creeping things physical discomfort is to be expected for a renuncian and the buddha always encouraged monks to go into seclusion to live up in the hills on the forests or in caves etc luxury is 1 of the obstacles to the spiritual path and it's always discouraged by the buddha it is also mentioned here that the monk submits to painful bodily feelings grievous sharp wrecking distracting and discomforting that drain the life away so here we see that when [Music] painful bodily feelings arise we should drain the life away the monk is supposed to bear to continue to live to practice the holy life um in other words even for anyone if such feelings arise it is better if we have not completed the goal the aim of the holy life that we embarrass such grievous pains and so that we can continue to practice and try to achieve the aim of the holy life which is the ending of the round of ebirds and finally the monk is a goal he goes to where he's never been before and in this long journey of samsara the round of rebirths namely to the ending of every basis for rebirth the destruction of craving to nibana so possessed of these 4 qualities such among is worthy of gifts and offerings of salutations with classic hands a field of merit unsurpassed for the world now he come to sudan number
